heading: Omitted
label: 2732 to 2736 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections were omitted in the general amendment of Part B of this subchapter by , , .Pub. L. 90–222, title I, § 10281 Stat. 682Dec. 23, 1967Section 2732, , , ; , , , provided for the formulation and implementation of programs for students of low-income families in need of earnings to allow them to stay in school and for individuals 16 through 21 years to develop maximum occupational potential.Pub. L. 88–452, title I, § 11278 Stat. 512Pub. L. 89–794, title I, § 112(a)80 Stat. 1454Aug. 20, 1964Nov. 8, 1966Section 2733, , , ; , , , set out the required conditions to allow payment of part or all of the costs of programs, including employment on public or locally sponsored projects, nonpolitical projects, and rates of pay, prohibited payment of wages for services on unauthorized projects, and provided for high priority projects.Pub. L. 88–452, title I, § 11378 Stat. 512Pub. L. 89–794, title I, § 112(a)80 Stat. 1454Aug. 20, 1964Nov. 8, 1966Section 2734, , , ; , , ; , (c), , , covered the eligibility of enrollees in program, Cuban refugees, non-applicability of Federal employment laws, and the use of public agencies and private organizations in the testing, counseling, job development, and referral services to youths.Pub. L. 88–452, title I, § 11478 Stat. 513Pub. L. 89–253, § 879 Stat. 974Pub. L. 89–794, title I, § 112(b)80 Stat. 1455Aug. 20, 1964Oct. 9, 1965Nov. 8, 1966Section 2735, , , ; , , ; , , , limited to 90 percent of the costs the Federal assistance in any program pursuant to this part with provisions for exceptions and allowed the non-Federal contribution to be in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated.Pub. L. 88–452, title I, § 11578 Stat. 513Pub. L. 89–253, § 979 Stat. 974Pub. L. 89–794, title I, § 112(d)80 Stat. 1455Aug. 20, 1964Oct. 9, 1965Nov. 8, 1966Section 2736, , , , required the Director to establish criteria in order to achieve equitable distribution among the States.Pub. L. 88–452, title I, § 11678 Stat. 513Aug. 20, 1964
